title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
After taking a ten o'clock flight, the narrator visits Hercule Poirot, who is drinking blackcurrant. The narrator informs Poirot that they are leaving the country soon and provides updates on the Crooked House case, including the news of another murder and a specific postcard. Poirot notes a pattern involving the numbers "4, 1, 3" (fourteen thirteen) and suggests the postcard is missing a bloody fingerprint. While Poirot refuses to name the killer, he claims to know the identity of the deceased man. To confirm his suspicions, Poirot instructs Miss Lemon to have a lawyer, Mr. Endicott, inquire about marriage records at Somerset House.

Meanwhile, ten days after September 20th, Inspector Hercule Hachette struggles with the unidentified deceased man's identity. Despite numerous leads from people claiming to recognize the man in a newspaper photo, investigations into the man's clothing and various local businesses have yielded nothing. Hachette receives a letter from a woman named Melina Rival, who believes the man is her long-lost husband, Harry. 

Inspector Grey introduces Mrs. Rival to Hachette. She appears to be around fifty years old due to heavy, casual makeup, with dyed reddish-brown hair, wearing a black coat, skirt, and white shirt. Mrs. Rival claims she last saw her husband about fifteen years ago on a train, though she notes they were married only three years ago. She states that Harry is an insurance agent, a profession she suspects may have been an excuse for him to be away from home for long periods.
